import CombinatorialGames.Game.Birthday

/-!
# Canonical games

If `y, z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `y < z` or if `y, z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z < y`, we say that `y` is **dominated** by `z`.
Under this assumption, the respective player will always choose to play on `z` rather than on `y`,
so we can remove `y` from consideration.

If `y ‚àà x·¥∏` and there exists some `z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z ‚â§ x`, or if `y ‚àà x·¥ø` and there exists some
`z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `x ‚â§ z`, we say that `x` is **reversible** through `y`. Under this assumption, if the
respective player plays on `y`, they can assume that the other player will then play on `z`, so they
must be ready to consider all left/right options of `z`, respectively. That is, if we remove `y` and
replace it by the left/right options of `z`, we'll get an equivalent game.

If `x` is short, we can define `undominate x` as an equivalent game where no subposition has
dominated moves. Likewise, for any `x`, we can define `unreverse x` as an equivalent game where no
subposition has reversible moves. Finally, for `x` short, we can define its **canonical form**
`canonical x = undominate (unreverse x)`, which will have no dominated and no reversible moves.

## Todo

- Prove that `x ‚âà y ‚Üí canonical x = canonical y`.
- Prove that `Game.birthday (.mk x) = IGame.birthday (canonical x)`.
-/

namespace IGame
variable {x y z : IGame.{u}} {p q : Player}

/-! ### Undominated games -/

/-- Removes all dominated positions from a game. This returns garbage values on non-short games. -/
def undominate (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{{y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥∏ ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
    {y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥ø ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}}
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_def : x.undominate =
    !{{y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
      {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}} := by
  rw [undominate]
  simp

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_left_undominate : x.undominate·¥∏ = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact leftMoves_ofSets ..

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_right_undominate : x.undominate·¥ø = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact rightMoves_ofSets ..

theorem mem_image_of_mem_moves_undominate (h : y ‚àà x.undominate.moves p) :
    y ‚àà undominate '' x.moves p := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  aesop

theorem birthday_undominate_le (x : IGame) : x.undominate.birthday ‚â§ x.birthday := by
  rw [undominate_def, birthday_le_iff']
  have (w) (hw : IsOption w x) := (birthday_undominate_le w).trans_lt (birthday_lt_of_isOption hw)
  aesop
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

instance [hx : Short x] : Short (undominate x) := by
  rw [short_iff_birthday_finite] at hx ‚ä¢
  exact ( birthday_undominate_le x).trans_lt hx

@[simp]
theorem undominate_neg (x : IGame) : (-x).undominate = -x.undominate := by
  have := fun p ‚Ü¶ moves_neg p x ‚ñ∏ Set.image_neg_of_apply_neg_eq_neg fun y _ ‚Ü¶ undominate_neg y
  rw [undominate_def, undominate_def]

    replace h := IH.trans_lt h

  ¬∑ rintro y ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, _‚ü©

termination_by x

theorem undominate_equiv (x : IGame) [Short x] : undominate x ‚âà x :=
  ‚ü®by simpa using le_undominate (-x), le_undominate x‚ü©

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated left moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  rw [moves_left_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' hy hz

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated right moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  rw [moves_right_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz

/-! ### Unreversible games -/

/-- A predicate for `y` to be a reversible move for `p` in `x`. -/
def Reversible (x y : IGame) (p : Player) : Prop :=
  p.cases (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x) (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z)

theorem reversible_left : Reversible x y left ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x := .rfl
theorem reversible_right : Reversible x y right ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z := .rfl

/-- Choose an arbitrary witness for the `Reversible` predicate. -/
def Reversible.choose (h : Reversible x y p) : IGame :=
  match p with | left | right => Classical.choose h

private theorem Reversible.choose_spec (h : Reversible x y p) :
    p.cases (h.choose ‚àà y·¥ø ‚àß h.choose ‚â§ x) (h.choose ‚àà y·¥∏ ‚àß x ‚â§ h.choose) := by
  cases p <;> exact Classical.choose_spec h

theorem Reversible.choose_mem_moves (h : Reversible x y p) : h.choose ‚àà y.moves (-p) := by
  cases p <;> exact h.choose_spec.1

theorem Reversible.choose_le (h : Reversible x y left) : h.choose ‚â§ x := h.choose_spec.2
theorem Reversible.le_choose (h : Reversible x y right) : x ‚â§ h.choose := h.choose_spec.2

open Classical in
/-- An auxiliary definition for `unreverse`. If `y` is a reversible move for `p` in `x`, then

@[aesop simp]
def reverseSet (x y : IGame) (p : Player) : Set IGame :=
  if h : Reversible x y p then h.choose.moves p else {y}

theorem reverseSet_of_reversible (h : Reversible x y p) : reverseSet x y p = h.choose.moves p :=
  dif_pos h

theorem reverseSet_of_not_reversible (h : ¬¨ Reversible x y p) : reverseSet x y p = {y} :=
  dif_neg h

instance : Small.{u} (reverseSet x y p) := by
  rw [reverseSet]
  split_ifs <;> infer_instance

theorem subposition_of_mem_reverseSet (hy : y ‚àà x.moves p) (h : z ‚àà reverseSet x y q) :
    Subposition z x := by
  rw [reverseSet] at h
  split_ifs at h with h'

  ¬∑ subst h
    exact .of_mem_moves hy

/-- Bypasses all reversible positions from a game and all its subpositions. -/
def unreverse (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{fun p ‚Ü¶ ‚ãÉ y : x.moves p, .range fun z : reverseSet x y p ‚Ü¶ unreverse z}
termination_by x

import CombinatorialGames.Game.Birthday

/-!
# Canonical games

If `y, z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `y < z` or if `y, z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z < y`, we say that `y` is **dominated**
by `z`.
Under this assumption, the respective player will always choose to play on `z` rather than on `y`,
so we can remove `y` from consideration.

If `y ‚àà x·¥∏` and there exists some `z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z ‚â§ x`, or if `y ‚àà x·¥ø` and there exists some
`z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `x ‚â§ z`, we say that `x` is **reversible** through `y`. Under this assumption, if the
respective player plays on `y`, they can assume that the other player will then play on `z`, so they
must be ready to consider all left/right options of `z`, respectively. That is, if we remove `y` and
replace it by the left/right options of `z`, we'll get an equivalent game.

If `x` is short, we can define `undominate x` as an equivalent game where no subposition has
dominated moves. Likewise, for any `x`, we can define `unreverse x` as an equivalent game where no
subposition has reversible moves. Finally, for `x` short, we can define its **canonical form**
`canonical x = undominate (unreverse x)`, which will have no dominated and no reversible moves.

## Todo

- Prove that `x ‚âà y ‚Üí canonical x = canonical y`.
- Prove that `Game.birthday (.mk x) = IGame.birthday (canonical x)`.
-/

universe u

noncomputable section

namespace IGame
variable {x y z : IGame.{u}} {p q : Player}

/-! ### Undominated games -/

/-- Removes all dominated positions from a game. This returns garbage values on non-short games. -/
def undominate (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{{y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥∏ ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
    {y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥ø ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}}
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_def : x.undominate =
    !{{y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
      {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}} := by
  rw [undominate]
  simp

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_left_undominate : x.undominate·¥∏ = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact leftMoves_ofSets ..

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_right_undominate : x.undominate·¥ø = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact rightMoves_ofSets ..

theorem mem_image_of_mem_moves_undominate (h : y ‚àà x.undominate.moves p) :
    y ‚àà undominate '' x.moves p := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  aesop

theorem birthday_undominate_le (x : IGame) : x.undominate.birthday ‚â§ x.birthday := by
  rw [undominate_def, birthday_le_iff']
  have (w) (hw : IsOption w x) := (birthday_undominate_le w).trans_lt (birthday_lt_of_isOption hw)
  aesop
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

instance [hx : Short x] : Short (undominate x) := by
  rw [short_iff_birthday_finite] at hx ‚ä¢
  exact ( birthday_undominate_le x).trans_lt hx

@[simp]
theorem undominate_neg (x : IGame) : (-x).undominate = -x.undominate := by
  have := fun p ‚Ü¶ moves_neg p x ‚ñ∏ Set.image_neg_of_apply_neg_eq_neg fun y _ ‚Ü¶ undominate_neg y
  rw [undominate_def, undominate_def]
  simp_all [IGame.lt_neg, IGame.neg_lt]
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

private theorem le_undominate (x : IGame) [Short x] : x ‚â§ undominate x := by
  rw [le_def, moves_left_undominate, moves_right_undominate]
  refine ‚ü®fun y hy ‚Ü¶ ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®z, ‚ü®hyz, ‚ü®hz, hz'‚ü©‚ü©‚ü© := (Short.finite_moves _ x).exists_le_maximal hy
    short
    have IH := le_undominate z
    refine .inl ‚ü®_, ‚ü®‚ü®Set.mem_image_of_mem _ hz, fun a ha h ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©, hyz.trans IH‚ü©‚ü©
    replace h := IH.trans_lt h
    exact (hz' ha h.le).not_gt h
  ¬∑ rintro y ‚ü®‚ü®z, ‚ü®hz, rfl‚ü©‚ü©, _‚ü©
    short
    exact .inr ‚ü®z, hz, le_undominate z‚ü©
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_equiv (x : IGame) [Short x] : undominate x ‚âà x :=
  ‚ü®by simpa using le_undominate (-x), le_undominate x‚ü©

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated left moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  rw [moves_left_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' hy hz

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated right moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  rw [moves_right_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' hy hz

/-! ### Unreversible games -/

/-- A predicate for `y` to be a reversible move for `p` in `x`. -/
def Reversible (x y : IGame) (p : Player) : Prop :=
  p.cases (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x) (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z)

theorem reversible_left : Reversible x y left ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x := .rfl
theorem reversible_right : Reversible x y right ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z := .rfl

/-- Choose an arbitrary witness for the `Reversible` predicate. -/
def Reversible.choose (h : Reversible x y p) : IGame :=
  match p with | left | right => Classical.choose h

private theorem Reversible.choose_spec (h : Reversible x y p) :
    p.cases (h.choose ‚àà y·¥ø ‚àß h.choose ‚â§ x) (h.choose ‚àà y·¥∏ ‚àß x ‚â§ h.choose) := by
  cases p <;> exact Classical.choose_spec h

theorem Reversible.choose_mem_moves (h : Reversible x y p) : h.choose ‚àà y.moves (-p) := by
  cases p <;> exact h.choose_spec.1

theorem Reversible.choose_le (h : Reversible x y left) : h.choose ‚â§ x := h.choose_spec.2
theorem Reversible.le_choose (h : Reversible x y right) : x ‚â§ h.choose := h.choose_spec.2

open Classical in
/-- An auxiliary definition for `unreverse`. If `y` is a reversible move for `p` in `x`, then
`reverseSet x y p` is defined as `p`'s moves in `Reversible.choose`. Otherwise,
`reverseSet x y p = {y}`. -/
@[aesop simp]
def reverseSet (x y : IGame) (p : Player) : Set IGame :=
  if h : Reversible x y p then h.choose.moves p else {y}

theorem reverseSet_of_reversible (h : Reversible x y p) : reverseSet x y p = h.choose.moves p :=
  dif_pos h

theorem reverseSet_of_not_reversible (h : ¬¨ Reversible x y p) : reverseSet x y p = {y} :=
  dif_neg h

instance : Small.{u} (reverseSet x y p) := by
  rw [reverseSet]
  split_ifs <;> infer_instance

theorem subposition_of_mem_reverseSet (hy : y ‚àà x.moves p) (h : z ‚àà reverseSet x y q) :
    Subposition z x := by
  rw [reverseSet] at h
  split_ifs at h with h'
  ¬∑ exact .trans (.trans (.of_mem_moves h) (.of_mem_moves h'.choose_mem_moves)) (.of_mem_moves hy)
  ¬∑ subst h

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_unreverse (x : IGame) (p : Player) : x.unreverse.moves p =
    ‚ãÉ y ‚àà x.moves p, unreverse '' reverseSet x y p := by

    (hy : y ‚àà x.moves p) (hz : z ‚àà h.choose.moves p) :

    unreverse y ‚àà x.unreverse.moves p := by

theorem birthday_unreverse_le (x : IGame) : x.unreverse.birthday ‚â§ x.birthday := by
  rw [unreverse_def, birthday_le_iff']
  simp_rw [IsOption, moves_ofSets, Set.mem_iUnion]
  rintro _ ‚ü®y, ‚ü®z, ‚ü®hz, ‚ü®w, hw, rfl‚ü©‚ü©‚ü©‚ü©
  have hw' := subposition_of_mem_reverseSet hz hw

import CombinatorialGames.Game.Birthday

/-!
# Canonical games

If `y, z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `y < z` or if `y, z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z < y`, we say that `y` is **dominated** by `z`.
Under this assumption, the respective player will always choose to play on `z` rather than on `y`,
so we can remove `y` from consideration.

If `y ‚àà x·¥∏` and there exists some `z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z ‚â§ x`, or if `y ‚àà x·¥ø` and there exists some
`z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `x ‚â§ z`, we say that `x` is **reversible** through `y`. Under this assumption, if the
respective player plays on `y`, they can assume that the other player will then play on `z`, so they
must be ready to consider all left/right options of `z`, respectively. That is, if we remove `y` and
replace it by the left/right options of `z`, we'll get an equivalent game.

If `x` is short, we can define `undominate x` as an equivalent game where no subposition has
dominated moves. Likewise, for any `x`, we can define `unreverse x` as an equivalent game where no
subposition has reversible moves. Finally, for `x` short, we can define its **canonical form**
`canonical x = undominate (unreverse x)`, which will have no dominated and no reversible moves.

-/

namespace IGame
variable {x y z : IGame.{u}} {p q : Player}

/-!### Undominated games -/

/-- Removes all dominated positions from a game. This returns garbage values on non-short games. -/
def undominate (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{{y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥∏ ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
    {y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥ø ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}}
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_def : x.undominate =
    !{{y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
      {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}} := by
  rw [undominate]
  simp

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_left_undominate : x.undominate·¥∏ = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact leftMoves_ofSets ..

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_right_undominate : x.undominate·¥ø = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact rightMoves_ofSets ..

theorem mem_image_of_mem_moves_undominate (h : y ‚àà x.undominate.moves p) :
    y ‚àà undominate '' x.moves p := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  aesop

theorem birthday_undominate_le (x : IGame) : x.undominate.birthday ‚â§ x.birthday := by
  rw [undominate_def, birthday_le_iff']
  have (w) (hw : IsOption w x) := (birthday_undominate_le w).trans_lt (birthday_lt_of_isOption hw)
  aesop
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

instance [hx : Short x] : Short (undominate x) := by
  rw [short_iff_birthday_finite] at hx ‚ä¢
  exact (birthday_udominate_le x).trans_lt hx

@[simp]
theorem undominate_neg (x : IGame) : (-x).undominate = -x.undominate := by
  have := fun p ‚Ü¶ moves_neg p x ‚ñ∏ Set.image_neg_of_apply_neg_eq_neg fun y _ ‚Ü¶ undominate_neg y
  rw [undominate_def, undominate_def]
  simp_all [IGame.lt_neg, IGame.neg_lt]
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

private theorem le_undominate (x : IGame) [Short x] : x ‚â§ undominate x := by
  rw [le_def, moves_left_undominate, moves_right_undominate]
  refine ‚ü®fun y hy ‚Ü¶ ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®z, ‚ü®hyz, ‚ü®hz, hz'‚ü©‚ü©‚ü© := (Short.finite_moves _ x).exists_le_maximal hy
    short
    have IH := le_undominate z
    refine .inl ‚ü®_, ‚ü®Set.mem_image_of_mem _ hz, fun a ha h ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©, hyz.trans IH‚ü©
    replace h := IH.trans_lt h
    exact (hz' ha h.le).not_gt h
  ¬∑ rintro y ‚ü®‚ü®z, ‚ü®hz, rfl‚ü©‚ü©, _‚ü©
    short
    exact .inr ‚ü®z, hz, le_undominate z‚ü©
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_equiv (x : IGame) [Short x] : undominate x ‚âà x :=
  ‚ü®by simpa using le_undominate (-x), le_undominate x‚ü©

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated left moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  rw [moves_left_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' hy hz

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated right moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  rw [moves_right_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' hy hz

/-!### Unreversible games -/

/-- A predicate for `y` to be a reversible move for `p` in `x`. -/
def Reversible (x y : IGame) (p : Player) : Prop :=
  p.cases (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x) (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z)

theorem reversible_left : Reversible x y left ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x := .rfl
theorem reversible_right : Reversible x y right ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z := .rfl

/-- Choose an arbitrary witness for the `Reversible` predicate. -/
def Reversible.choose (h : Reversible x y p) : IGame :=
  match p with | left | right => Classical.choose h

private theorem Reversible.choose_spec (h : Reversible x y p) :
    p.cases (h.choose ‚àà y·¥ø ‚àß h.choose ‚â§ x) (h.choose ‚àà y·¥∏ ‚àß x ‚â§ h.choose) := by
  cases p <;> exact Classical.choose_spec h

import CombinatorialGames.Game.Birthday

/-!
# Canonical games

If `y, z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `y < z` or if `y, z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z < y`, we say that `y` is **dominated** by `z`.
Under this assumption, the respective player will always choose to play on `z` rather than on `y`,
so we can remove `y` from consideration.

If `y ‚àà x·¥∏` and there exists some `z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z ‚â§ x`, or if `y ‚àà x·¥ø` and there exists some
`z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `x ‚â§ z`, we say that `x` is **reversible** through `y`. Under this assumption, if the
respective player plays on `y`, they can assume that the other player will then play on `z`, so they
must be ready to consider all left/right options of `z`, respectively. That is, if we remove `y` and
replace it by the left/right options of `z`, we'll get an equivalent game.

If `x` is short, we can define `undominate x` as an equivalent game where no subposition has
dominated moves. Likewise, for any `x`, we can define `unreverse x` as an equivalent game where no
subposition has reversible moves. Finally, for `x` short, we can define its **canonical form**
`canonical x = undominate (unreverse x)`, which will have no dominated and no reversible moves.

## Todo

- Prove that `x ‚âà y ‚Üí canonical x = canonical y`.
- Prove that `Game.birthday (.mk x) = IGame.birthday (canonical x)`.
-/

universe u

noncomputable section

namespace IGame
variable {x y z : IGame.{u}} {p q : Player}

/-! ### Undominated games -/

/-- Removes all dominated positions from a game. This returns garbage values on non-short games. -/
def undominate (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{{y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥∏ ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
    {y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥ø ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}}
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_def : x.undominate =
    !{{y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
      {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}} := by
  rw [undominate]
  simp

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_left_undominate : x.undominate·¥∏ = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact leftMoves_ofSets ..

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_right_undominate : x.undominate·¥ø = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact rightMoves_ofSets ..

theorem mem_image_of_mem_moves_undominate (h : y ‚àà x.undominate.moves p) :
    y ‚àà undominate '' x.moves p := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  aesop

theorem birthday_undominate_le (x : IGame) : x.undominate.birthday ‚â§ x.birthday := by
  rw [undominate_def, birthday_le_iff']
  have (w) (hw : IsOption w x) := (birthday_undominate_le w).trans_lt (birthday_lt_of_isOption hw)
  aesop
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

instance [hx : Short x] : Short (undominate x) := by
  rw [short_iff_birthday_finite] at hx ‚ä¢
  exact (birthday_udominate_le x).trans_lt hx

@[simp]
theorem undominate_neg (x : IGame) : (-x).undominate = -x.undominate := by
  have := fun p ‚Ü¶ moves_neg p x ‚ñ∏ Set.image_neg_of_apply_neg_eq_neg fun y _ ‚Ü¶ undominate_neg y
  rw [undominate_def, undominate_def]
  simp_all [IGame.lt_neg, IGame.neg_lt]
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

private theorem le_undominate (x : IGame) [Short x] : x ‚â§ undominate x := by
  rw [le_def, moves_left_undominate, moves_right_undominate]
  refine ‚ü®fun y hy ‚Ü¶ ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®z, ‚ü®hyz, ‚ü®hz, hz'‚ü©‚ü©‚ü© := (Short.finite_moves _ x).exists_le_maximal hy
    short
    have IH := le_undominate z
    refine .inl ‚ü®_, ‚ü®‚ü®Set.mem_image_of_mem _ hz, fun a ha h ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©, hyz.trans IH‚ü©‚ü©
    replace h := IH.trans_lt h
    exact (hz' ha h.le).not_gt h
  ¬∑ rintro y ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü©
    short
    exact .inr ‚ü®z, hz, le_undominate z‚ü©
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_equiv (x : IGame) [Short x] : undominate x ‚âà x :=
  ‚ü®by simpa using le_undominate (-x), le_undominate x‚ü©

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated left moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  rw [moves_left_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' hy hz

/-- `undominate x` has no dominated right moves. -/
theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate [Short x]
    (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  rw [moves_right_undominate] at hz
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®z, hz, rfl‚ü©, hz'‚ü© := hz
  short
  grw [undominate_equiv z]
  exact not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' hy hz

/-! ### Unreversible games -/

/-- A predicate for `y` to be a reversible move for `p` in `x`. -/
def Reversible (x y : IGame) (p : Player) : Prop :=
  p.cases (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x) (‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z)

theorem reversible_left : Reversible x y left ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥ø, z ‚â§ x := .rfl
theorem reversible_right : Reversible x y right ‚Üî ‚àÉ z ‚àà y·¥∏, x ‚â§ z := .rfl

/-- Choose an arbitrary witness for the `Reversible` predicate. -/
def Reversible.choose (h : Reversible x y p) : IGame :=
  match p with | left | right => Classical.choose h

private theorem Reversible.choose_spec (h : Reversible x y p) :
    p.cases (h.choose ‚àà y·¥ø ‚àß h.choose ‚â§ x) (h.choose ‚àà y·¥∏ ‚àß x ‚â§ h.choose) := by
  cases p <;> exact Classical.choose_spec h

theorem Reversible.choose_mem_moves (h : Reversible x y p) : h.choose ‚àà y.moves (-p) := by
  cases p <;> exact h.choose_spec.1

theorem Reversible.choose_le (h : Reversible x y left) : h.choose ‚â§ x := h.choose_spec.2
theorem Reversible.le_choose (h : Reversible x y right) : x ‚â§ h.choose := h.choose_spec.2

import CombinatorialGames.Game.Birthday

/-!
# Canonical games

If `y, z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `y < z` or if `y, z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z < y`, we say that `y` is **dominated** by `z`.
Under this assumption, the respective player will always choose to play on `z` rather than on `y`,
so we can remove `y` from consideration.

If `y ‚àà x·¥∏` and there exists some `z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z ‚â§ x`, or if `y ‚àà x·¥ø` and there exists some
`z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `x ‚â§ z`, we say that `x` is **reversible** through `y`. Under this assumption, if the
respective player plays on `y`, they can assume that the other player will then play on `z`, so they
must be ready to consider all left/right options of `z`, respectively. That is, if we remove `y` and
replace it by the left/right options of `z`, we'll get an equivalent game.

If `x` is short, we can define `undominate x` as an equivalent game where no subposition has
dominated moves. Likewise, for any `x`, we can define `unreverse x` as an equivalent game where no
subposition has reversible moves. Finally, for `x` short, we can define its **canonical form**
`canonical x = undominate (unreverse x)`, which will have no dominated and no reversible moves.

## Todo

- Prove that `x ‚âà y ‚Üí canonical x = canonical y`.
- Prove that `Game.birthday (.mk x) = IGame.birthday (canonical x)`.
-/

noncomputable section

namespace IGame
variable {x y z : IGame.{u}} {p q : Player}

/-! ### Undominated games -/

/-- Removes all dominated positions from a game. This returns garbage values on non-short games. -/
def undominate (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{{y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥∏ ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
    {y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥ø ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}}
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_def : x.undominate =
    !{{y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
      {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}} := by
  rw [undominate]
  simp

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_left_undominate : x.undominate·¥∏ = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact leftMoves_ofSets ..

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_right_undominate : x.undominate·¥ø = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact rightMoves_ofSets ..

theorem mem_image_of_mem_moves_undominate (h : y ‚àà x.undominate.moves p) :
    y ‚àà undominate '' x.moves p := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_left_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥∏) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥∏) : ¬¨y < z := by
  aesop

theorem not_lt_of_mem_moves_right_undominate' (hy : y ‚àà x.undominate·¥ø) (hz : z ‚àà x·¥ø) : ¬¨z < y := by
  aesop

theorem birthday_undominate_le (x : IGame) : x.undominate.birthday ‚â§ x.birthday := by
  rw [undominate_def, birthday_le_iff']
  have (w) (hw : IsOption w x) := (birthday_undominate_le w).trans_lt (birthday_lt_of_isOption hw)
  aesop
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

instance [hx : Short x] : Short (undominate x) := by
  rw [short_iff_birthday_finite] at hx ‚ä¢
  exact (birthday_undominate_le x).trans_lt hx

@[simp]
theorem undominate_neg (x : IGame) : (-x).undominate = -x.undominate := by
  have := fun p ‚Ü¶ moves_neg p x ‚ñ∏ Set.image_neg_of_apply_neg_eq_neg fun y _ ‚Ü¶ undominate_neg y

import CombinatorialGames.Game.Birthday

/-!
# Canonical games

If `y, z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `y < z` or if `y, z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z < y`, we say that `y` is **dominated** by `z`.
Under this assumption, the respective player will always choose to play on `z` rather than on `y`,
so we can remove `y` from consideration.

If `y ‚àà x·¥∏` and there exists some `z ‚àà x·¥ø` with `z ‚â§ x`, or if `y ‚àà x·¥ø` and there exists some
`z ‚àà x·¥∏` with `x ‚â§ z`, we say that `x` is **reversible** through `y`. Under this assumption, if the
respective player plays on `y`, they can assume that the other player will then play on `z`, so they
must be ready to consider all left/right options of `z`, respectively. That is, if we remove `y` and
replace it by the left/right options of `z`, we'll get an equivalent game.

If `x` is short, we can define `undominate x` as an equivalent game where no subposition has
dominated moves. Likewise, for any `x`, we can define `unreverse x` as an equivalent game where no
subposition has reversible moves. Finally, for `x` short, we can define its **canonical form**
`canonical x = undominate (unreverse x)`, which will have no dominated and no reversible moves.

## Todo

- Prove that `x ‚âà y ‚Üí canonical x = canonical y`.
- Prove that `Game.birthday (.mk x) = IGame.birthday (canonical x)`.
-/

universe u

noncomputable section

namespace IGame
variable {x y z : IGame.{u}} {p q : Player}

/-!### Undominated games -/

/-- Removes all dominated positions from a game. This returns garbage values on non-short games. -/
def undominate (x : IGame) : IGame :=
  !{{y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥∏ ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
    {y ‚àà Set.range fun z : x·¥ø ‚Ü¶ undominate z | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}}
termination_by x
decreasing_by igame_wf

theorem undominate_def : x.undominate =
    !{{y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} |
      {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y}} := by
  rw [undominate]
  simp

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_left_undominate : x.undominate·¥∏ = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥∏ | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥∏, ¬¨y < z} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact leftMoves_ofSets ..

@[aesop simp]
theorem moves_right_undominate : x.undominate·¥ø = {y ‚àà undominate '' x·¥ø | ‚àÄ z ‚àà x·¥ø, ¬¨z < y} := by
  rw [undominate_def]
  exact rightMoves_ofSets ..

theorem mem_image_of_mem_moves_undominate (h : y ‚àà x.undominate.moves p) :
    y ‚àà undominate '' x.moves

rw [moves_unreverse, Set.mem_iUnion‚ÇÇ] at hy
